text:
(a) A motor vehicle may not be the subject of a subsequent sale at a public auction by a holder of a dealer’s general distinguishing number unless equitable or legal title has passed to the selling dealer before the transfer of title to the subsequent buyer.

(b) The holder of a dealer’s general distinguishing number who sells a motor vehicle at a public auction must transfer the certificate of title for that vehicle to the buyer before the 21st day after the date of the sale.
markdown:
# Transp. Code
## Title 7
### Chapter 503. Dealer's & Mfr.'s Vehicle License Plates
##### § 503.039 — Public Motor Vehicle Auctions

(a) A motor vehicle may not be the subject of a subsequent sale at a public auction by a holder of a dealer’s general distinguishing number unless equitable or legal title has passed to the selling dealer before the transfer of title to the subsequent buyer.

(b) The holder of a dealer’s general distinguishing number who sells a motor vehicle at a public auction must transfer the certificate of title for that vehicle to the buyer before the 21st day after the date of the sale.

*Acts 2011, 82nd Leg., R.S., Ch. 1290 (H.B.2017), Sec. 29, eff. September 1, 2011.*

subsections.label: a
subsections.text: A motor vehicle may not be the subject of a subsequent sale at a public auction by a holder of a dealer’s general distinguishing number unless equitable or legal title has passed to the selling dealer before the transfer of title to the subsequent buyer.

subsections.label: b
subsections.text: The holder of a dealer’s general distinguishing number who sells a motor vehicle at a public auction must transfer the certificate of title for that vehicle to the buyer before the 21st day after the date of the sale.

history.raw: Acts 2011, 82nd Leg., R.S., Ch. 1290 (H.B.2017), Sec. 29, eff. September 1, 2011.
